title: Oxygen: The Rise
review:
Honestly, didn't love this. I felt like I was watching some 'best of' YouTube compilation - clips from their TV appearances cut together, with the dances slotted in between. There was no story other than 'this is what we've done,' no dramaturgy, no real attempt to pull the individual works into something cohesive. I almost fell asleep... 

I was also left with more questions than I went in with: what happened to her health? Can she still perform? (Evidently, yes, so why was this brought up and not addressed properly?). 

It just shows you can bring all the dance talent in the world together, but without a story, it can fall flat. Especially when you're used to companies like Dutch National Ballet, ISH, or DNB Junior Company's Grimm, where every piece has a reason for existing.
full_description: THE RISE is an explosive performance full of powerful standalone numbers that follow one another at breakneck speed — an ode to choreographer Jennifer Romen's impressive oeuvre: a celebration of growth, courage and undeniable artistic strength. Iconic choreographies, special collaborations and new energy come together in a show that feels like both coming home and moving forward at the same time. Blacklight, shadow play and projections make intangible illusions tangible. Not an ending, but the beginning of a new chapter.
ai_summary: Dutch hip-hop and contemporary dance company Oxygen — winners of Dance As One and Golden Buzzer on France's Got Talent — mark a new chapter with The Rise: a high-energy retrospective of Jennifer Romen's most powerful work, built with blacklight illusions and unstoppable momentum.

extension.music_notes: Music by Subp Yao; includes works by Ludovico Einaudi
